# Verify `sesam user change-groups` and `sesam user rename` write the right
# audit entries and update the verified state, and that the log stays valid.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Add bob as a dev.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev
exec sesam user list
stdout 'bob'

# Change bob's groups: dev -> dev, ops.
exec sesam user change-groups --user bob --group dev --group ops
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.change_groups"'
exec sesam log
stdout 'set groups of bob to dev, ops'
exec sesam verify

# Before the rename bob's signing key lives under his old name.
exists .sesam/signkeys/bob.age
! exists .sesam/signkeys/robert.age

# Rename bob to robert.
exec sesam user rename bob robert
exec sesam show .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
stdout '"user.rename"'
stdout '"robert"'
exec sesam log
stdout 'renamed user bob → robert'
exec sesam verify

# The signing key file is renamed too: bob.age is gone, robert.age exists.
exists .sesam/signkeys/robert.age
! exists .sesam/signkeys/bob.age

# After the rename only robert is known, bob is gone.
exec sesam user list
stdout 'robert'
! stdout '\bbob\b'

# A non-existent user cannot be renamed.
! exec sesam user rename ghost phantom
